textPlain: > Even minor papers by the most eminent scientists are cited much more than papers by relatively unknown scientistsI wonder if this is because a paper with such a citation is likely to be taken more seriously than a citation that might actually be more relevant.

> the opposing "Newton hypothesis", which says that scientific progress is mostly the work of a relatively small number of great scientists (after Isaac Newton's statement that he "stood on the shoulders of giants")I guess the Ortega equivalent statement would be "I stood on top of a giant pile of tiny people"...Not quite as majestic, but hey, if it gets the job done...
